commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed; this active implementation PR has no blocking review finding and needs normal maintainer review/check handling.; Cleared: The diff only changes Feishu channel routing arguments and does not touch dependencies, workflows, credentials, package metadata, or supply-chain execution paths. (sha=8b01486c6760711ba0c08481e66e2eb721e7d48c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an optional Feishu reply-dispatcher `sendTarget`, passes computed `feishuTo` from both dispatcher call sites, and uses it for visible sends while preserving raw `chatId` for lifecycle state.

PR surface: Source +10. Total +10 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main computes `user:<open_id>` for Feishu DMs but still passes raw `ctx.chatId` into the dispatcher while non-thread DM reply metadata is suppressed. I did not run a live current-main Feishu tenant repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed; this active implementation PR has no blocking review finding and needs normal maintainer review/check handling.

target.title: [Bug] Fix Feishu p2p DM replies fail with SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND (fixes #83730)
target.body:
## Summary

**What problem does this PR solve?**

Non-thread Feishu p2p (DM) replies are routed to `chat:<oc_xxx>` (the raw p2p chat ID) instead of `user:<open_id>`, causing `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` errors when the bot attempts to reply.

**Why does this matter now?**

Issue #83730 is a P1 regression introduced in OpenClaw 2026.5.12. After `skipReplyToInMessages` suppresses reply metadata for non-thread DMs, the visible reply becomes a top-level send to the `oc_*` chat target — which Feishu rejects.

**What is the intended outcome?**
- Non-thread p2p DM replies are sent to `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`
- Thread p2p DM replies and group replies are unaffected
- Streaming start and conversation type detection keep using `chatId` as before

**What is intentionally out of scope?**
- No changes to other channels or the Feishu send/target resolution layer

## Linked context

Closes #83730

## Real behavior proof — live Feishu test

Real environment tested: commit 8b01486c, local OpenClaw gateway + Feishu bot (appId cli_a9f668faaab89bd1, WebSocket mode)

Two p2p DM tests were performed. Key Gateway logs:

```
2026-06-19T16:30:42.334 [feishu] received message from ou_REDACTED in oc_REDACTED (p2p)
2026-06-19T16:30:42.343 [feishu] Feishu[default] DM from ou_REDACTED: 你好你好你好
2026-06-19T16:30:42.348 [feishu] dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:main)
2026-06-19T16:30:59.555 [feishu] dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)

2026-06-19T16:31:11.217 [feishu] received message from ou_REDACTED in oc_REDACTED (p2p)
2026-06-19T16:31:11.222 [feishu] Feishu[default] DM from ou_REDACTED: 你是哪里人？
2026-06-19T16:31:11.225 [feishu] dispatching to agent (session=agent:main:main)
2026-06-19T16:31:21.116 [feishu] dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
```

Both replies delivered successfully. No `SUBSCRIPTION_NOT_FOUND` errors in any log line.

**Observed result after fix:** The AI replied in both cases. Chat screenshot in comments.

**What was not tested:** Group chat and thread DM replies (same code path, expected unaffected).

**Proof limitations:** The bot Feishu app lacks `contact:contact.base:readonly` scope (stale, non-blocking). Streaming cards fall back to non-streaming delivery due to a pre-existing card creation HTTP 400 (unrelated to this dispatch target fix).

## Tests and validation
- reply-dispatcher.test.ts — 82 passed
- bot.test.ts — 87 passed
- channel.test.ts + send.test.ts + send-target.test.ts — 84 passed

## Risk checklist
**User-visible behavior change:** Yes — non-thread Feishu p2p DM replies now use `user:<open_id>` instead of `chat:<oc_xxx>`
**Config/env/migration/security change:** No
**Highest risk:** The `to:` target change affects all visible sends. Mitigated by `sendTarget ?? chatId` fallback.
target.author: xydigit-zt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
